HTTP logging: nestjs-pino (LoggerModule) + app.useLogger(Logger) in main.ts.
Config: node `config` package merged into ConfigService (examples/nest-js pattern).
Abstract base for Pino-backed app logging (nestjs-example LoggingModule pattern).
Global BasePinoLogger via nestjs-pino (agent-rules-n-skills examples/nest-js pattern).
Mirrors `config/llm` — sampling for Mastra; HF/ctx/cache align with local llama.cpp runs.
Context length (llama-cli `--ctx-size`); informational for ops — set on the inference server.
Cache / HF home hint (env `LLAMA_CACHE`); used when running llama-cli locally, not by Nest.

You are a playful programming assistant.

Your only job is to reply with a short, self-contained programming-related joke.

Rules:
- Ignore the user prompt content completely.
- Always respond with a single, brief joke (1–3 sentences).
- Do not ask follow-up questions or reference external files/tools.

parent: Publishing `@zyreth/agasi` to npm/After publish — install for users
Publishing `@zyreth/agasi` to npm/After publish — install for users

text: cd packages/agasi && npm install && npm run build && npm link

agasi init
agasi source add rules ~/Documents/agent-rules-n-skills/skills
agasi target add --id myrepo --source rules --repo ~/path/to/repo --dest cursor --dest agents
agasi install
agasi doctor

agasi sync
agasi sync --prune   # drop symlinks for removed skills

version: 1
sources:
  - name: rules
    path: ~/path/to/skills
targets:
  - id: app
    source: rules
    repoRoot: ~/path/to/repo
    destinations:
      - type: cursor
      - type: agents

npm install
npm run build
npm test
npm run docs:dev     # VitePress dev server (edit docs/*.md)
npm run docs:build   # static site → docs/.vitepress/dist/
npm run docs:preview # preview the built site
Symlink flat agent skills (SKILL.md) into Cursor, .agents, Codex, and custom paths — one global config.

cd packages/agasi
npm run pack:dry    # inspect tarball contents
npm version patch   # or minor / major (if using git tags from package dir)
npm publish --access public

parent: Publishing to npm/Deploying the static doc site

text:
version: 1

sources:
  - name: rules
    path: /Users/you/Documents/agent-rules-n-skills/skills

targets:
  - id: rules-repo
    source: rules
    repoRoot: /Users/you/Documents/agent-rules-n-skills
    destinations:
      - type: cursor
      - type: agents

  - id: codex-global
    source: rules
    repoRoot: ""
    destinations:
      - type: codex

agasi source add …
agasi target add …

agasi init
agasi source add rules ~/path/to/skills
agasi target add --id myapp --source rules --repo ~/path/to/repo --dest cursor --dest agents
agasi install
agasi doctor

cd packages/agasi
npm install
npm run build
npm link
agasi --help

cd packages/agasi
npm install
npm run docs:build

agasi target add --id codex-global --source rules --dest codex
agasi install --target codex-global

text:
agasi target add --id app --source rules --repo ~/projects/my-app --dest cursor --dest agents
agasi target add --id codex --source rules --dest codex

targets:
  - id: my-app
    source: rules
    repoRoot: /abs/path/to/repo
    destinations:
      - type: cursor
      - type: agents

destinations:
  - type: cursor
  - type: agents
  - type: codex
  - type: path
    relative: tools/my-skills

targets:
  - id: codex
    source: rules
    repoRoot: ""
    destinations:
      - type: codex

Git worktrees, gitignore-aware env mirroring, and VS Code / Cursor workspace helper CLI.

parent: Configuration/Example: legacy single `.env` at worktree root

text:
cd packages/taskasi
npm install
npm run build
npm link

# >>> taskasi
# Local per-task workspace copies (template file stays tracked).
subscriptions-*.workspace
subscriptions-*.code-workspace
# <<< taskasi

taskasi switch 1587
taskasi switch 1587 --no-env
taskasi switch 1587 --no-workspace

npm run test:integration
# or: npm run test:all
Legacy: single `env.source` file copied to worktree root as `.env`.
Create git worktrees and optional VS Code workspace copies
Print config file path and effective defaults
Add a worktree for task id (branch = prefix + id)
Do not copy .env into the worktree
Do not write a workspace copy or touch .gitignore
taskasi: no env files matched discovery rules (env mirror skipped)
taskasi: workspace enabled but no template found (expected {basename}.workspace or {basename}.code-workspace in repo or parent; set workspace.source to override)
taskasi: .gitignore updated (taskasi block: ignore per-task workspace copies)
If set, copy only this file into the worktree root as `.env` (legacy). If unset, discover env files (see README).
Extra basenames to always mirror (exact match), in addition to `.env` / `.env.*`.
If true (default), also copy files ignored by gitignore when the matching rule looks env-related (e.g. `*.env`).
Extra directory names to skip when walking (always skips `.git`, `node_modules`, `dist`, etc., and `worktreeRelativeDir`).
Resolved worktree folder name is empty; check worktreeFolderTemplate

True if a .gitignore rule line (pattern) is treating this as an env-style file.
Avoid matching unrelated rules (e.g. `.envrc` pattern should not match `.env` substring loosely).
For relative paths (posix), returns paths that are ignored by git with an env-related pattern.
status 0: at least one path ignored; 1: none ignored — both may still print matches on stdout for 0 only
Could not resolve git repository root
# Local per-task workspace copies (template file stays tracked).

Ensures repo `.gitignore` contains a taskasi-managed section that ignores
`{basename}-*.workspace` and `{basename}-*.code-workspace` so the canonical
`basename.workspace` / `basename.code-workspace` template is not matched.
@returns true if `.gitignore` was created or modified
Absolute path to global config file. Override with env `TASKASI_CONFIG_PATH` (tests, sandboxes).
Workspace JSON has no folders array
Workspace folder entry missing string path
finds .workspace in repo when code-workspace missing
finds .env and nested .env.local but skips node_modules
Writable under package (works in restricted CI sandboxes that block system temp).

Integration tests against a real `git` binary (init, worktree).
Not included in default `npm test` — run `npm run test:integration` or `npm run test:all`.
Requires a normal environment (e.g. Cursor **open shell** / full permissions) so `git init`
can create `.git/hooks`; restricted sandboxes often block this.
localBranchExists is false for unknown branch
addWorktree creates new branch and path
writes rewritten JSON next to template shape
rewrites root and in-repo paths; preserves ../
